name: Dmytro Kirpa
education: Master’s Degree, Computer Software Engineering from Zhytomyr State Technological University
headline: Senior Software Engineer | Design Systems, UI Platforms & Developer Experience @ Microsoft
about:
Engineering leader and Senior Software Engineer with 15+ years of experience building and scaling design systems, frontend platforms, and developer infrastructure in large organizations.

At Microsoft, I work on the Fluent UI ecosystem — the company’s main UI library used across hundreds of products by thousands of engineers. My focus is on designing and evolving shared UI systems that are performant, accessible, and easy to adopt. I’ve led initiatives that reduced memory usage by ~50% through virtualization, enabled server-side rendering in core components, and unblocked major platform migrations that improved initial render times from ~1.2s to under 200ms.

I build tooling and AI-ready documentation pipelines that turn design system knowledge into usable context for internal coding agents and AI-assisted workflows, improving discoverability, onboarding, and long-term maintainability.

Previously, as a Principal Engineer, Tech Lead, and Engineering Manager, I’ve owned frontend platforms end-to-end — from architecture and governance to cross-team rollout and developer enablement.

I’m driven by complex systems problems at scale and enjoy building platforms that reduce fragmentation, raise engineering standards, and help organizations ship consistent, high-quality user experiences with confidence.

currentPositions.description:
•	Core contributor to the Fluent UI ecosystem, the main UI library used across hundreds of products by thousands of engineers.
	•	Led development and stabilization of shared React components, styling infrastructure, and accessibility integrations.
	•	Reduced memory consumption by ~50% in a high-traffic chat application by introducing and integrating virtualized message lists.
	•	Enabled server-side rendering (SSR) support in design system components, unblocking platform migration and improving initial render time from ~1.2s to under 200ms.
	•	Built internal tooling to analyze, index, and structure Storybook documentation for AI-assisted workflows, enabling Fluent UI context to be consumed by internal coding agents.
	•	Supported large-scale migrations (React 17 → 18 → 19, ESM-only packages, SSR/RSC compatibility).
	•	Acted as technical point-of-contact for multiple partner teams adopting Fluent UI.

experiences.title: Principal Front-End Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
•	Led frontend platform modernization across multiple esports and media products.
	•	Designed and evolved shared UI components and frontend architecture guidelines.
	•	Partnered with design and product leadership to ensure consistent UX.
	•	Mentored senior engineers and tech leads on platform best practices

experiences.title: Front-End Tech Lead
experiences.jobDescription:
•	Led frontend architecture and design system adoption across core SaaS products.
	•	Designed and maintained reusable component libraries used by multiple teams.
	•	Established frontend governance model (standards, reviews, documentation).
	•	Built internal tooling and onboarding workflows to improve developer productivity.
	•	Reduced feature delivery time through standardized UI patterns.
	•	Defined and executed technical roadmap for frontend platform evolution.
	•	Mentored engineers and grew senior technical leadership.

experiences.title: Engineering Manager
experiences.jobDescription:
•	Built and led frontend engineering team, setting technical vision and delivery standards.
	•	Defined frontend platform strategy and multi-quarter technical roadmap.
	•	Introduced modern React-based architecture and development workflows.
	•	Established strong collaboration between design, product, and engineering.
	•	Reduced technical debt and improved delivery predictability.
	•	Coached engineers and supported career growth.

experiences.title: Front-End Tech Lead
experiences.jobDescription:
•	Led frontend feature development and architectural decisions.
	•	Established shared UI patterns and coding standards.
	•	Reviewed code and mentored engineers.
	•	Contributed to company-wide engineering initiatives.

experiences.title: Engineering Team Lead
experiences.jobDescription:
Collaborate with the team to deliver a fabulous end-user experience
Lead and encourage standardization of standard methodologies in front-end code
Ensure broad adoption of the best principles through collaboration and active listening
Cultivate an atmosphere of quality by championing testing standard methodologies and engineering efficiency

experiences.title: Senior Frontend Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Mobile and Web Platform is built on the Liferay Portal framework and enriched custom-developed CMS features. The platform offers a joint solution for mobile and web
clients and is delivered as a box portal product or as part of a complex customer-tailored solution.

Tasks performed:
Collaborated with teammates and wrote new code that meets product requirements
Maintained existing well-formulated code and library components
Reviewed other team members' code

experiences.title: Front-End Engineer
experiences.jobDescription: Collaborate with team members to develop code that meets product requirements, maintain existing code and libraries, and reviewcode written byother team members.

recommendationsReceived.name: Yevhen Lepekha
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Front End Team Lead - Playtech
recommendationsReceived.context: Yevhen worked with Dmytro on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: Dmytro is very strong frontend developer with great technical skills. He always is open for new technologies and knowledge sharing. With Dmytro we have developed gambling solutions on the same project in Playtech. It was my big pleasure working with Dmytro.

recommendationsReceived.name: Oleh Okolsky
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Games Delivery Manager | 200+ Games Annually & 1200+ Live Titles | 12+ Years in Game Dev & iGaming | Building Top-Tier, Efficient & Scalable Delivery Models
recommendationsReceived.description: Dmitry is wonderful to work with, and has unique expertise in our domain. He has a very strong work ethic and unparalleled analytical and problem solving skills. Dmitry is dedicated, self-motivated, methodical, and very capable. He had vision to see the benefits to turn that into a competitive advantage for our games. Definitely someone I'd want to have on my team.

recommendations.name: Mila Terzi
recommendations.subtitle: iGaming,COO, CEO deputy
recommendations.context: Mila was senior to Dmytro but didn't manage Dmytro directly
recommendations.description: I can recommend Mila as a person with great education and deep proficiency of business solutions. Always takes care of the job, works hard and really enjoys team-working. She denotes an easiness to build interpersonal relations with others. Has natural ability to manage people.

recommendations.name: Oleh Okolsky
recommendations.subtitle: Games Delivery Manager | 200+ Games Annually & 1200+ Live Titles | 12+ Years in Game Dev & iGaming | Building Top-Tier, Efficient & Scalable Delivery Models
recommendations.description: Oleh is not only a highly motivated and dedicated project manager but also an inspiring perfectionist. I can recommend Oleh as a person with great background and deep recognition of modern solutions. Oleh is a reliable, customer-service oriented and detail oriented professional, a person you can trust when you want to be sure that important tasks will be completed in a systematic and precise way. Deadline oriented team player, with great sense of humor. For Oleh the job is always the most important thing to do.

summary: Dmytro Kirpa is currently a Microsoft engineer working on the Fluent UI React ecosystem and the emerging GenUI platform that blends LLMs with modern web UX; he also publishes updates tied to People.ai, a growth-stage/unicorn company that recently closed a $100M Series D. His posts routinely cover intersections of AI and front-end engineering—React, component libraries, design systems, and LLM-powered developer tooling—while also switching to startup milestones, product launches, hiring calls, and personal or humanitarian updates. The tone alternates between technical and recruiting (detailed, product-focused descriptions of projects and roles) and more personal, reflective moments that show leadership, appreciation, and real-world impact. He emphasizes practical engineering work—building a unified design-system knowledge base, model evaluation pipelines, and cross-disciplinary collaboration—while signaling a strong product and platform mindset. Notable patterns include a mix of deep technical subject matter (generative UX, React components, model evaluation) with community-oriented content (job postings, funding announcements, product promotions) and occasional socially conscious commentary. Overall, his content differentiates itself by bridging enterprise-scale UI engineering at Microsoft with startup growth experience, positioning him as a practitioner who connects AI/LLM innovation to usable, design-driven front-end platforms.
